title: feat: Dual Patched App support (ie: 2 G/Photos App with different settings)
author: SubZero5
body:
### Feature description

I wish to have 2 patched G/Photos APK that work with my 2 accounts.


### Motivation

I want open 1st G/Photos App and use my 1st Google account for it with it's own folder backup settings; then I want to open 2nd G/Photos App and use my 2st Google account for it with it's own separate folder backup settings.

Current whenever I switch accounts all my prior selected folders are backed up regardless of which I want separately... 

### Acknowledgements

- [x] I have checked all open and closed feature requests and this is not a duplicate
- [x] I have chosen an appropriate title.
- [x] All requested information has been provided properly.
- [x] The feature request is only related to ReVanced Manager.

title: bug: Instagram patcher is partially working?
author: sasunaru95
body:
### Bug description

I can get the Instagram patcher on revanced to go through maybe up to 5-7 patches before it craps the bed. It then decides that a patch it was allowing to work on a previous run is no longer valid and null. I got every single patch except the universal patches and the outdated build patch to work on the URV patcher so I dont think it is the patches for the most part (I am patching 417.0.0.54.77 btw). 

I think this is a different bug than what most people are reporting cuz from what I see nobody can even get one patch to hit. I was getting several to run through before hitting a wall.

body: ### Bug description Seems like every patch breaks with the same error. ### Patch logs ```shell App: com.instagram.android Source: ReVanced Patches Added: Anonymous story viewing, Disable Reels auto-scroll, Disable story auto flipping, Enable developer menu, Hide navigation buttons, Open links externally Removed: Remove build expired popup Manager version: 2.6.0 Manager auto-update: true Allow changing patch selection: true Use process runtime: true Process runtime custom memory limit: 4096MB Memory limit: 256MB (large: 512MB) Root permissions: No RAM: 3.01 GB / 11.65 GB available Storage: 62.21 GB / 234 GB available Android version: 16 (API 36) Supported architectures: arm64-v8a Model: SM-S938B ``` ### Debug logs [revanced-manager_logcat_2026-07-11T09_39_45.801442.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29924443/revanced-manager_logcat_2026-07-11T09_39_45.801442.txt) ### Acknowledgements - [x] I have checked all open and closed bug reports and this is not a duplicate. - [x] I have chosen an appropriate title. - [x] All requested information has been provided properly. - [x] The bug is only related to ReVanced Manager.

title: bug: YouTube Music pauses after exactly 1 minute on non-root device
author: chiranjibbiswal
body:
### Bug description

After patching and installing YouTube Music with ReVanced Manager, playback always pauses after exactly 1 minute. Regular YouTube ReVanced works normally. I uninstalled and reinstalled YouTube Music, but the problem persists.

title: bug: GmsCore conflict with stock GMS causes crashes in prebuilt ReVanced YouTube packages
author: Puatikskao
body:
### Bug description _Hello, there is a significant problem with apps that use revanced gms on android conflicting with native google play services. The issue is that using morphe works fine as a standalone package, but anddea and youtube revanced experience conflicts between gapps and revanced.gms, which causes widespread crashes throughout google play services. This is a report I prepared with the help of claude. I am not a programmer, so I have no actual expertise, but I managed to troubleshoot the issue with claude._

Title

GmsCore conflict with stock GMS causes crashes in prebuilt ReVanced YouTube packages

Environment

Device | Pixel 9 Pro (caiman) -- | -- ROM | crDroid 12.5 Build fingerprint | google/caiman/caiman:16/BP3A.251105.015 GMS (stock) | com.google.android.gms 26.24.34 (NikGapps) GmsCore | app.revanced.android.gms 25.28.37

Secondary symptoms

  • Play Store reports no internet connection (connectivity confirmed working)
  • YouTube Studio hangs indefinitely on load

Steps to reproduce

  1. Install crDroid on a Pixel 9 Pro with NikGapps (stock GMS present)
  2. Install prebuilt ReVanced YouTube packages via ReVanced Manager — specifically anddea.youtube and app.revanced.android.youtube
  3. ReVanced Manager installs app.revanced.android.gms (GmsCore) alongside the existing stock GMS
  4. Launch either affected YouTube package → app crashes immediately
  5. Observe Play Store reporting no internet connection and YouTube Studio hanging on load

Patches / downloader / settings

The affected packages were installed as prebuilts via ReVanced Manager and do not expose their patch configuration. It is not possible to determine which specific patches were applied or whether GmsCore support was explicitly enabled. The working package (app.morphe.android.youtube) is also a prebuilt with no visible patch configuration, but does not install or depend on GmsCore, suggesting a difference in how each prebuilt was originally compiled.

Root cause (observed)

Two GMS providers coexist on the device: stock GMS installed via NikGapps and GmsCore installed by ReVanced Manager. The prebuilt packages anddea.youtube and app.revanced.android.youtube appear to bind to GmsCore at runtime. When stock GMS is also present, service binding conflicts cause both packages to crash and destabilise other Google services on the device.

Suggested fix

Prebuilt packages distributed via ReVanced Manager that include a GmsCore dependency should either (a) detect the presence of stock GMS and fall back gracefully, or (b) clearly document that they are incompatible with devices running stock GMS so users on standard Gapps ROMs can make an informed choice before installation.


_Hopefully this is useful. currently I am running morphe because the other two do not work._ ### Patch logs ```shell ``` ### Debug logs . ### Acknowledgements - [x] I have checked all open and closed bug reports and this is not a duplicate. - [x] I have chosen an appropriate title. - [x] All requested information has been provided properly. - [x] The bug is only related to ReVanced Manager.

title: bug: Shorts player shows black screen on Samsung A70 with/without Spoof video streams patch
author: joshwilsonwill-boop
body:
### Bug description

**Device:** Samsung Galaxy A70 (SM-A7050)
**Android:** 11 (API 30)
**YouTube version:** 20.40.45
**ReVanced Manager:** 2.6.0
**Patches version:** 6.2.1

**Problem:**
Shorts player displays a black screen — no video frames render. The UI shell loads correctly (title, channel name, like/comment/share buttons, subscribe button), but the actual video content is blank. Regular long-form videos play perfectly fine.

**Steps to reproduce:**
1. Open ReVanced YouTube
2. Navigate to Shorts
3. Tap any Short
4. Black screen, no video frames

**Patches applied:**
- Spoof video streams: ✅ (required for regular video playback)
- Spoof device dimensions: ✅
- Spoof app version: ✅
- Hide ads, Return YouTube Dislike, SponsorBlock, etc.

**What I've tried:**
- Unchecking Spoof device dimensions + Spoof app version: Shorts still broken
- Regular videos work fine with Spoof video streams enabled

**Note:**
The in-app popup mentioned playback issues were fixed in the latest patches, but this Shorts-specific issue persists.

### Debug logs

## Bug Description
Shorts player displays a black screen — no video frames render. The UI shell loads correctly (title, channel name, like/comment/share buttons), but the actual video content is blank. Regular long-form videos play perfectly fine.

## Device Info
- **Device:** Samsung Galaxy A70 (SM-A7050)
- **Android:** 11 (API 30)
- **YouTube version:** 20.40.45
- **ReVanced Manager:** 2.6.0
- **Patches version:** 6.2.1

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Open ReVanced YouTube
2. Navigate to Shorts
3. Tap any Short
4. Black screen, no video frames

## Patches Applied
- Spoof video streams: ✅ (required for regular video playback)
- Spoof device dimensions: ✅
- Spoof app version: ✅
- Hide ads, Return YouTube Dislike, SponsorBlock, etc.

## What I've Tried
- Unchecking Spoof device dimensions + Spoof app version: Shorts still broken
- Regular videos work fine with Spoof video streams enabled

## Expected Behavior
Shorts should play video content normally, not show a black screen.

title: bug: Already downloaded apps not showing up in the settings
author: syrupderg
body:
### Bug description

Already existing downloaded apps in `/data/data/app.revanced.manager.flutter/app_downloaded-apps/` does not show up in the "Apps" list in ReVanced settings -> Downloads -> Apps

Root access and a 3rd party file explorer is needed to remove those downloaded apps, for users with no root it is currently impossible to clear it without completely removing the app and reinstalling it, clearing the cache does not get rid of those files.

title: feat: Make it Easier to see the Recommended Version for the App
author: SheltieChan
body:
### Feature description

There needs to be a way to see what the recommended version of an app is so that I can see if I'm able to update the version of my patched app.

This was available in older versions of the patcher, but seems to have disappeared in the redesign, as (Using YouTube for an example) the Youtube App just shows up as "YouTube Revanced" instead of "YouTube".  This only shows me the version number of my patched YouTube app, and I cannot find Regular YouTube among the apps list.  This means I cannot check to see if there's a newer recommended version for the YouTube App that I can update to.

Please return this, or explain what I'm misunderstanding, because as it stands I have no idea when I'm meant to update my app or what version I'm meant to update to.

### Motivation

It's important because it makes the experience of patching an app/updating your patch of an app way easier in terms of knowing what and when you're meant to update.

Even something as simple as a list of recommended versions for apps somewhere would solve this.

### Acknowledgements

- [x] I have checked all open and closed feature requests and this is not a duplicate
- [x] I have chosen an appropriate title.
- [x] All requested information has been provided properly.
- [x] The feature request is only related to ReVanced Manager.

title: feat: Include SplitAPK functionality.
author: FURTEK-SV
body:
### Feature description

So years ago with APK mirror and APK pillar you used to just be able to get apks without a bundle. It seems these days you are forced to get a bundle for almost every app. It seems that revance manager can't handle the bundles and it will forever loop. There is an app titled Anti-Split M. I was wondering if you could collab. It allows extraction of the APK from a bundle. 

### Motivation

Collaborating with this functionality would mean that APK pure and APK mirror would still be able to provide the files but the app will be able to deal with them without the need for an external system. 

### Acknowledgements

- [x] I have checked all open and closed feature requests and this is not a duplicate
- [x] I have chosen an appropriate title.
- [x] All requested information has been provided properly.
- [x] The feature request is only related to ReVanced Manager.

title: bug: Controlling YouTube Music with Gemini through Android Auto Reports that Premium Subscription is Required
author: RecursiveRegistrations
body:
### Bug description

I am attempting to use a patched version of YouTube Music 8.40.54 on a rooted phone. I am using mount mode for my installation. When using the app through the phone, it works perfectly. The problem is with Android Auto. If I select music on the phone, it plays fine through Android Auto and I am able to seek back and forth between songs, but if I try to ask Gemini to play something, it responds with a message stating that it cannot play it because I do not have a premium license. The exact wording varies with each request attempt but the meaning is the same.

I have a recorded a demo showing how the issue looks. In the recording, you cannot hear my voice but you can hear how Gemini responds to my request.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b6a7876d-a0f2-4f1b-8dbd-51923aede79f

title: bug:  Each video buffers for 2 minutes before starting, Playback speed option issues, and more....
author: HoneyRoll
body:
### Bug description

1. Each video keeps taking **at least** 2 minutes to start (Internet connection is stable)
2. Playback speed options above 2x are no longer available. The only option "available" is the 3x speed (but it is not actually available; in fact, a prompt keeps popping up stating I have to pay for YouTube Premium).
3. Video transcription and timeline are unavailable or keeps saying "Tap to retry" with everything blank
4. Revanced Manager 2.6.0 (latest release, can't install anything above)
5. Youtube version 20.40.45 (latest version that is compatible with current patches)
6. Already tried reinstalling and repatching with **even worse** results...
7. Many countless patches no longer seem to be working or recognized
8. Random buffering
9. After pausing video in miniplayer mode (overlay while doing other things) and pressing the play button to resume, video often goes from speed above 1.0x (example: 2.0x) back down to 1.0x
10. The page (after you click the "Library" icon at the bottom) looks drastically different and not user friendly (thumbnails of playlists and "Watch History" don't show up like before)
11. Doesn't easily let you switch between YouTube accounts and users like before (the "Switch account" feature and profile icon are also no longer available in the "Library" page, you literally have click "Home" button icon at the bottom, and click the profile icon on the top right to "Switch account").
12. Video quality above 144p / 240p / 360p triggers endless buffering (especially when the video is live) and defaults to 360p or lower (especially after it says "Could not fetch any streams") 
13. "Could not fetch any streams" and other error playback-related messages have become constant 
14. Buffering becomes worse when speed is anywhere above 1.0x
15. On Sunday, June 21, 2026, I received more than 1 notification from Revanced Manager that a new version of Revanced Manager was available (v2.6.0), and it kept failing to install, receiving error messages like "Couldn't install update", when I checked why, it was when I realized that I already have version 2.6.0 installed, but apparently, Revanced Manager forgot and didn't realize it and also doesn't realize that it is currently on Planet Earth...
16. Whenever it buffers, exiting and restarting the app and device don't work; neither does pausing video, tapping / sliding the seekbar to go to any part of the video, or restarting the video playback; doesn't matter what video or channel it is, also doesn't matter if the video is live, not live, uploaded, etc.
17. It has become nearly impossible to watch live videos due to endless buffering no matter what I do to adjust the settings, the buffering still happens even when using default settings.
18. Before it stopped working completely, many of the videos wouldn't load or play, and the ones that did load, if I skipped to any part of the video (especially anywhere closer to the middle or end of the video), it wouldn't play, and I had to let it play from the very beginning every time I'd watch/re-watch a video, let it buffer 30 seconds in for several seconds, and then it would continue to play and sometimes me skip around; and also if the video was somehow paused, it wouldn't unpause. The videos would often stop working and there would be playback errors with either a frozen video frame with the buffering circle wheel, black screen, green screen, or whatever else (no error ID seen).
19. I tried disabling and enabling spoof video settings in Revanced > Misc > Spoof Video Streams and changing the default client from Android Reel to Android VR, and still nothing worked.


**UPDATE (Thursday, June 25, 2026):** 
ReVanced App / YouTube no longer working. Impossible to fetch any streams. Also been getting a lot of "Sign in to confirm you're not a bot" messages (and I'm not even using a VPN) network-wide on all devices and browsers around the same time I re-installed and re-patched the ReVanced YouTube app. 

I'm updating the post as I go and notice any additional issues.
